evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onetbb to parallelize a CPU loop that writes to a shared histogram.
evals.expected_output: Does not blindly use shared mutation in parallel_for; proposes partition-local aggregation or a safe concurrent pattern, plus correctness and benchmark checks.

evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onetbb to choose a oneTBB pattern for a three-stage image pipeline with bounded I/O and CPU-heavy processing.
evals.expected_output: Considers parallel_pipeline or flow graph, explains concurrency limits, ordering, blocking I/O concerns, and validation.

evals.prompt: Use $uxl-onetbb to diagnose slower performance after adding oneTBB inside an app that already owns a thread pool.
evals.expected_output: Focuses on oversubscription, arenas/global_control, nested parallelism, thread limits, affinity, and benchmark evidence.
